entity.summary: Incorporate a private company limited by shares — ACRA requires at least one director. Having raised S$150,000 from a recognised regional VC fund, VietStack clears the EntrePass innovator track's venture-funding bar (at least S$100,000 from a single round from an eligible investor), so the founders can apply for an EntrePass alongside incorporation rather than needing a separate Employment Pass sponsor from day one.
entity.callout: S$150,000 raised clears the S$100,000 EntrePass venture-funding threshold with room to spare.
nextSteps: Appoint at least one director to satisfy ACRA's incorporation requirement.
File the EntrePass innovator-track application citing the VC funding round.

visaCompass.summary: VietStack relocates 10 PMETs in total (2 founders, 8 staff) — under the 25-PMET small-firm threshold — so C3 (Diversity) and C4 (Support for local employment) both default to the neutral 10-point score instead of being scored on actual hiring mix. That's the small-firm nuance: a young team isn't penalised for not yet having a diverse or majority-local workforce. Combined with an assumed qualifying non-financial-services salary (C1: 20) and a recognised degree (C2: 10), the illustrative total is 50 points, clearing the 40-point pass threshold.
visaCompass.callout: Money moment: below 25 PMETs, diversity and local-hiring scoring defaults to neutral — VietStack passes today on that basis, not because it's already diverse.
nextSteps: Confirm the actual offered salary and applicant qualifications before filing — C1/C2 here assume a qualifying benchmark, not a real candidate.
Plan hiring diversity and local-hiring support well before headcount reaches 25 PMETs, since the neutral default disappears at that point.

licenses.summary: SaaS doesn't have a single named licence in the knowledge base — the government's licence finder needs to be checked against VietStack's exact business activity before operating, since requirements vary by the specific service offered.
nextSteps: Run VietStack's specific SaaS activity through the GoBusiness licence finder before launch.

taxIncentives.summary: Standard Singapore tax facts apply: 17% flat corporate rate, the first-three-years start-up exemption (75% of the first S$100,000, 50% of the next S$100,000), and 9% GST. Because VietStack is setting up as a regional HQ, it's also a candidate for EDB's Development and Expansion Incentive — a discretionary concessionary 5-15% tax rate on qualifying regional/global HQ income once operations are established. At a projected S$1,200,000 in Singapore revenue, VietStack is also above the S$1 million GST registration threshold.
taxIncentives.callout: Regional-HQ setups like VietStack's can apply to EDB for a concessionary tax rate on qualifying HQ income — approval isn't automatic.
nextSteps: Register for GST ahead of the S$1 million threshold already being crossed.
Scope an EDB Development and Expansion Incentive application once regional-HQ operations are established.

banking.summary: As a foreign-owned company, VietStack should expect enhanced due diligence and a longer account-opening review — DBS requires ACRA incorporation documents, signatory identity/address verification, and board resolutions up front.
nextSteps: Prepare ACRA documents and signatory ID/address verification before the first bank meeting.

timeline.summary: Roughly a 6-week runway: incorporate in week 1, run the SaaS activity through the licence finder in week 2, open the bank account in week 3 (padded for foreign-owned due diligence), submit the EntrePass and staff Employment Pass applications in week 4, and register for GST in week 6 given projected revenue already exceeds the S$1 million threshold.

riskMatrix.summary: Three risks stand out: a foreign-owned-company bank account delay (medium/medium); the COMPASS diversity flag that will apply once headcount crosses 25 PMETs, since today's neutral C3/C4 scoring is a small-firm default, not a permanent one (low/medium); and GST registration exposure, since projected revenue already exceeds the S$1 million threshold (medium/medium).
nextSteps: Register for GST now rather than waiting for the retrospective test to trigger it.
Start planning nationality-mix and local-hiring practices well ahead of the 25-PMET mark.
